source: Official Journal of the European Union — EU AI Act (regulation text)
v0_9_3_note: Tier bumped D -> A (article-level text matches the authoritative regulation). Annex III risk-category annotations added per v0.9.3 P2.1. Sub-article granularity deferred to v0.9.4 SME pass.

controls.title: Prohibited AI practices
controls.description: Prohibited AI practices (social scoring by public authorities, real-time biometric identification in public spaces, subliminal manipulation, exploitation of vulnerabilities, untargeted facial scraping, emotion inference in workplace/education).

controls.title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Classification of AI systems as high-risk under Annex III, plus exemptions for systems performing narrow procedural tasks, preparatory work, or detection of decision patterns.

controls.title: Risk management system
controls.description: Risk management system established, implemented, documented, and maintained across the AI system lifecycle.

controls.title: Data and data governance
controls.description: Training, validation, and testing datasets meet quality criteria (relevance, representativeness, appropriate statistical properties, absence of errors).

controls.title: Technical documentation
controls.description: Technical documentation drawn up before the high-risk AI system is placed on the market or put into service; kept up to date.

controls.title: Record-keeping (logs)
controls.description: Automatic recording of events ('logs') over the lifecycle of the high-risk AI system to ensure traceability.

controls.title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
controls.description: Design and development such that operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret outputs and use appropriately.

controls.title: Human oversight
controls.description: Effective human oversight by natural persons during the period of use; tools enabling the oversight built in.

controls.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
controls.description: Design and development to achieve appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity over the lifecycle; resilience to errors, faults, inconsistencies, and attempts to alter inputs/outputs.

controls.title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Providers' obligations: compliance, QMS, documentation, automatic logs, conformity assessment, registration in EU database, post-market monitoring.

controls.title: Quality management system
controls.description: Quality management system in place ensuring compliance; documented in written form.

controls.title: Documentation keeping
controls.description: Documentation kept at disposal of national competent authorities for 10 years after the system is placed on the market.

controls.title: Automatically generated logs
controls.description: Logs automatically generated by the high-risk AI system kept by the provider for an appropriate period (>= 6 months).

controls.title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Deployers' use of the system in accordance with provider's instructions, oversight, input data quality, log retention, registry checks.

controls.title: Fundamental rights impact assessment (FRIA)
controls.description: Public-sector deployers and certain private deployers of Annex III systems conduct a fundamental rights impact assessment before deployment.

controls.title: Transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems
controls.description: Disclosure to natural persons interacting with AI systems (50.1), notification of emotion recognition (50.3), labelling of synthetic/deepfake content (50.4).

controls.title: Classification of general-purpose AI models as GPAI models with systemic risk
controls.description: GPAI models exceeding compute threshold (10^25 FLOPs) presumed to have systemic risk; Commission may designate others.

controls.title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
controls.description: GPAI providers: technical documentation, copyright policy, training-data summary, downstream-provider info.

controls.title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk
controls.description: Additional obligations for systemic-risk GPAI: model evaluation, adversarial testing, risk assessment + mitigation, incident reporting, cybersecurity.

controls.title: Post-market monitoring system
controls.description: Providers establish and document a post-market monitoring system proportionate to risks.

controls.title: Reporting of serious incidents
controls.description: Providers report serious incidents to market surveillance authorities of Member States within set deadlines (15 days; 2 days for widespread infringement; 10 days for serious + irreversible disruption).

controls.title: Penalties
controls.description: Administrative fines up to EUR 35M or 7% of total worldwide annual turnover for Article 5 violations; lower tiers for other infringements.
